description_long: This is a cylindrical stone block with a flat top and bottom, featuring a smooth, slightly weathered surface. Around its upper side, there is a continuous geometric band with a repeating angular pattern, rendered in a slightly darker shade of gray-green. The body of the block has a subtly faceted, chiseled texture, giving it a hand-crafted appearance. The top surface is mostly smooth with faint surface irregularities, while the sides show more pronounced textural variation. The coloration is predominantly gray with hints of green, suggesting either mineral content or mild surface moss. This object is robust and solid, likely used as a barrier or decorative element in urban or landscaped environments.
description: A cylindrical, gray stone block with a geometric band and chiseled texture, showing mild weathering and hints of green.
description_view_0: The front view shows the geometric band clearly and highlights the chiseled texture on the lower half.
description_view_1: This side view is similar to the front, with the geometric band and surface texture visible, but the lighting shifts the shadowing slightly.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, maintaining the visibility of the geometric band and the faceted stone texture.
description_view_3: The final view continues to show the geometric band and chiseled surface, with minor differences in shadow and highlight.
attribute_installation_method: set on a leveled concrete or compacted gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Mild surface weathering with faint greenish discoloration, possibly from moss or lichen; no major chips or cracks visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A continuous geometric band is engraved around the upper side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears isolated but could be aligned with similar blocks as a barrier or decorative border.
